title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated virtual environment and install all required packages for CDHR experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch with CUDA: pip install torch==2.5.1 torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install vLLM for efficient inference: pip install vllm==0.6.4
steps.step4: Install transformers and related: pip install transformers==4.46.3 accelerate sentencepiece protobuf
steps.step5: Install datasets and evaluation: pip install datasets==2.21.0 evaluate sympy
steps.step6: Install sentence embeddings for retrieval: pip install sentence-transformers==3.3.0
steps.step7: Install analysis/visualization: pip install numpy scipy matplotlib seaborn pandas scikit-learn
steps.step8: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step9: Create project structure: mkdir -p src data results figures logs

title: Download and Preprocess Datasets
description: Download all benchmark datasets, create train/validation/test splits, and build analogical retrieval index

steps.step4: Download AIME 2024 problems (manual scrape or use available HF datasets)
steps.step5: Extract first 100 GSM8K train problems for validation set (threshold selection and assumption validation)
steps.step6: Create retrieval index from GSM8K+MATH training sets: encode problems using all-MiniLM-L6-v2
steps.step7: Save embeddings to data/retrieval_index.pkl with FAISS index for fast similarity search
steps.step8: Compute dataset statistics: problem lengths, answer distributions, difficulty levels
steps.step9: Create data manifest file with all splits and file paths

title: Download and Setup Language Models
description: Download all three base models and verify they fit in GPU memory

steps.step1: Download Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct from HuggingFace (requires HF token)
steps.step4: Test load each model with vLLM: verify <40GB VRAM usage per model
steps.step5: Create model configuration JSON with model paths, context lengths, and default generation parameters
steps.step6: Verify all models can generate with test prompts and produce coherent outputs

title: Baseline 1: Standard Chain-of-Thought
description: Run standard single-pass CoT on all datasets with all models as the simplest baseline

steps.step1: Implement standard CoT prompt: 'Let's solve this step by step...'
steps.step3: Run on GSM8K test set (1319 problems) for all 3 models
steps.step4: Run on MATH500 (500 problems) for all 3 models
steps.step5: Run on GPQA Diamond (198 problems) for all 3 models
steps.step6: Run on AIME 2024 (~30 problems) for all 3 models
steps.step7: Extract final answers using regex patterns for each dataset format
steps.step8: Record accuracy, tokens used, and latency per problem
steps.step9: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) for stochasticity analysis (seeds affect sampling when temperature>0)

title: Baseline 2: Self-Consistency with 16 Samples
description: Run self-consistency baseline with 16 parallel samples and majority voting as a strong baseline

steps.step1: Use same CoT prompt but with temperature=0.7 for diversity
steps.step2: Generate 16 samples per problem using vLLM's parallel generation
steps.step3: Extract answers from all 16 outputs and apply majority voting
steps.step4: Record Pass@1 (best single), Pass@16 (any correct), and Consensus accuracy
steps.step5: Track total tokens (16x overhead) and latency
steps.step6: Run on subset: GSM8K (first 300 for speed), MATH500 (full), GPQA (full), AIME (full)
steps.step7: Use single seed (42) due to high compute cost

title: Pre-Validation: Confidence Dynamics Assumptions
description: Validate core theoretical assumptions on held-out set before full CDHR implementation (addresses feedback point 2)

steps.step1: Select 100 problems from GSM8K validation set (not used in main experiments)
steps.step2: Run Llama-3.1-8B with step-by-step CoT, recording confidence at each step
steps.step3: Compute confidence using token entropy: c_token = exp(mean(log_probs))
steps.step4: Compute confidence velocity: v_t = (c_t - c_{t-3}) / 3 with window=3
steps.step5: Compute confidence variance: sigma_t^2 = Var(c_{t-2}, c_{t-1}, c_t)
steps.step6: Annotate trajectory types based on thresholds: Progressing (v>0.05, sigma<0.1), Stagnant (|v|<0.05), Oscillating (sigma>0.1), Declining (v<-0.05)
steps.step7: Verify Hypothesis: Progressing trajectories correlate with correct steps (precision >= 75%)
steps.step8: Verify Hypothesis: Declining trajectories correlate with errors (recall >= 70%)
steps.step9: Verify Hypothesis: Oscillating trajectories occur 2x more on hard problems (bottom 50% accuracy)
steps.step10: Document results; if assumptions fail, adjust thresholds or abort with failure report

title: Threshold Selection via Grid Search
description: Empirically validate and select optimal threshold values for theta_v and theta_sigma (addresses feedback point on empirical justification)

steps.step1: Use same 100-problem validation set from pre-validation
steps.step3: For each (theta_v, theta_sigma) pair, classify all reasoning steps in validation set
steps.step4: Compare classification against human-annotated reasoning states (or proxy: final correctness)
steps.step5: Compute trajectory-classification accuracy for each threshold pair
steps.step6: Select theta_v=0.05, theta_sigma=0.1 as defaults (should align with theoretical expectations)
steps.step7: Record the grid search results showing these are near-optimal

title: CDHR: Confidence-Dynamic Heterogeneous Reasoning
description: Run full CDHR method on all datasets with all models

steps.step1: Implement confidence estimation: composite score with beta=0.5 (balanced token + consistency)
steps.step2: Implement trajectory monitoring: velocity and variance with window=3
steps.step3: Implement strategy selection: Progressing→Linear, Stagnant→Analogical, Oscillating→Decomposition, Declining→Verification
steps.step4: Implement strategy prompts with state passing between switches
steps.step5: Implement analogical retrieval with relevance verification (similarity threshold 0.75) and fallback
steps.step6: Implement safeguards: OOD detection, calibration check, max 5 switches per problem
steps.step7: Run CDHR on GSM8K (1319 problems) for all 3 models with 3 seeds each (42, 123, 456)
steps.step8: Run CDHR on MATH500 (500 problems) for all 3 models with 3 seeds each
steps.step9: Run CDHR on GPQA Diamond (198 problems) for all 3 models with 3 seeds each
steps.step10: Run CDHR on AIME 2024 (~30 problems) for all 3 models with 3 seeds each
steps.step11: Record: accuracy, tokens per problem, number of strategy switches, strategy distribution, latency
steps.step12: Log detailed traces for 50 random problems per dataset for analysis

title: Baseline 3: Iterative Self-Refinement (CoRefine-style)
description: Implement a simplified self-refinement baseline that uses confidence to trigger rethinking (stronger baseline)

steps.step1: Implement iterative refinement: if confidence < 0.5, regenerate with 'Let me reconsider' prompt
steps.step2: Max 3 iterations, early stop if confidence increases above threshold
steps.step3: Run on all datasets with Llama-3.1-8B (primary comparison model)
steps.step4: Use single seed (42) due to compute constraints
steps.step5: Record accuracy, tokens, iterations per problem

title: Ablation 1: Strategy Component Ablation
description: Test impact of each reasoning strategy by removing them one at a time

steps.step1: CDHR-LinearOnly: Remove analogical and decomposition, use only Linear+Verification
steps.step2: CDHR-NoAnalogical: Keep Linear+Decomposition+Verification, skip analogical transfer
steps.step4: Run all variants on GSM8K subset (first 300 problems) with Llama-3.1-8B
steps.step6: Compare against full CDHR on same subset

title: Ablation 2: Confidence Signal Ablation
description: Test different confidence signal combinations (addresses feedback on sensitivity analysis)

steps.step2: CDHR-ConsistencyOnly: Use only self-consistency (beta=0.0, k=5 samples)
steps.step3: Run both on GSM8K subset (first 300 problems) with Llama-3.1-8B
steps.step4: Compare accuracy, tokens, and latency against full CDHR (beta=0.5)

title: Ablation 3: Beta Sensitivity Analysis
description: Analyze sensitivity to beta parameter in composite confidence score (addresses feedback point 5)

steps.step2: For each beta, run CDHR on GSM8K subset (first 200 problems) with Llama-3.1-8B
steps.step3: Record accuracy and tokens for each beta value
steps.step4: Plot accuracy vs beta and tokens vs beta
steps.step5: Identify optimal or robust beta range

title: Ablation 4: Threshold Sensitivity Analysis
description: Test robustness to threshold variations around selected values

steps.step3: Run all 9 combinations on GSM8K subset (first 200 problems) with Llama-3.1-8B
steps.step4: Record accuracy and stability of results
steps.step5: Report whether performance is sensitive to threshold choices

title: Ablation 5: Dynamics Features Ablation
description: Test using only velocity vs only variance vs both

steps.step1: CDHR-VelocityOnly: Use only velocity for strategy selection (ignore variance)
steps.step2: CDHR-VarianceOnly: Use only variance for strategy selection (ignore velocity)
steps.step3: Run both on GSM8K subset (first 200 problems) with Llama-3.1-8B
steps.step4: Compare against full CDHR with both features

title: Analogical Retrieval Quality Analysis
description: Evaluate retrieval quality and fallback mechanism effectiveness (addresses feedback point 4)

steps.step1: For all problems where Analogical Transfer was triggered, record retrieval similarity scores
steps.step4: Compute relevance rate: proportion of retrievals with similarity > 0.75
steps.step5: Compare accuracy when fallback triggered vs when analogical was used
steps.step6: Verify relevance rate >= 70% target (secondary success criterion)

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Compute statistical tests to verify improvements are significant

steps.step1: Aggregate results across all 3 seeds for CDHR and baselines
steps.step2: Compute mean and standard error for accuracy on each dataset
steps.step3: Perform paired t-test between CDHR and Standard CoT on each dataset
steps.step4: Perform paired t-test between CDHR and Self-Consistency (where applicable)
steps.step6: Check if primary success criteria are met with statistical significance

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify all success criteria are met based on experimental results

steps.step1: Primary criterion: Check if CDHR achieves accuracy within 2% of best baseline with >=30% lower latency, OR outperforms CoT by >=5% with <=20% token overhead
steps.step2: Secondary criterion: Compute entropy of strategy distribution, verify > 0.5 bits
steps.step3: Secondary criterion: Compute precision of confidence dynamics predicting correctness, verify >= 80%
steps.step4: Secondary criterion: Check generalization - performance consistent across all 3 model families
steps.step5: Secondary criterion: Verify analogical retrieval relevance rate >= 70%
steps.step6: Document which criteria passed/failed with numerical evidence
steps.step7: If criteria not met, analyze failure modes as documented in proposal

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create all figures and tables for the research paper

steps.step1: Figure 1: Main results bar chart - accuracy comparison across methods and datasets
steps.step2: Figure 2: Accuracy-Latency Pareto frontier showing efficiency trade-offs
steps.step3: Figure 3: Strategy distribution pie chart showing adaptation behavior
steps.step4: Figure 4: Confidence trajectory examples (correct vs incorrect reasoning)
steps.step5: Figure 5: Ablation study results showing component contributions
steps.step6: Figure 6: Beta sensitivity curve (accuracy vs beta value)
steps.step8: Table 1: Main results table with all metrics (accuracy, tokens, latency) across all datasets
steps.step10: Table 3: Strategy selection frequency by problem difficulty
steps.step11: Save all figures to figures/ directory in high-resolution PNG and PDF formats

title: Final Report Generation
description: Compile comprehensive experiment report with all results

steps.step1: Generate summary JSON with all key metrics: accuracies, tokens, latencies, ablation results
steps.step2: Create detailed experiment log documenting any issues or deviations from plan
steps.step3: Write findings report summarizing whether hypotheses were supported
steps.step4: Document any negative results or unexpected findings
